applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel with optimized block size
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented depthwise conv2d Triton kernel with BLOCK_SIZE=512. The key optimization was using a large block size (512) to reduce kernel launch overhead and improve occupancy on AMD MI355X. The kernel uses a 3D grid (batch, channels, height*width_blocks) where each thread block processes 512 output width elements in parallel. This achieves 1.48x speedup over PyTorch reference.

attempted_but_failed.name: Smaller block sizes (128, 256)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Higher kernel launch overhead, worse performance

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=1024
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slightly worse than 512 (6.01ms vs 5.83ms), possibly due to register pressure

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile wrapper
attempted_but_failed.reason: Produced incorrect output despite faster timing

attempted_but_failed.name: Flat grid with division
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break statements in loops

notes: Depthwise conv2d is memory-bound with arithmetic intensity ~1.8 FLOPs/byte
PyTorch reference uses optimized rocBLAS/hipBLASLt kernels at 8.62ms
Key insight: BLOCK_SIZE=512 gives best performance by balancing occupancy and register usage
AMD MI355X has wavefront size 64, so block sizes should be multiples of 64
Triton kernel correctness requires matching weight initialization exactly - used nn.Conv2d for this
